title: Debugging Practice
instructions: Find and fix the bugs in the provided functions. Each function has a logical error that needs to be corrected.
hints: calculate_average: Check if the total is actually being updated in the loop
remove_duplicates: Modifying a list while iterating causes issues. Try creating a new list.
is_palindrome: Consider case sensitivity and spaces. 'Race Car' should be a palindrome.

testCases.description: calculate_average should correctly compute the mean of a list of numbers
calculate_average should work with a single element
remove_duplicates should remove duplicates while preserving order
testCases.validation: assert remove_duplicates([1, 2, 2, 3, 1, 4]) == [1, 2, 3, 4], 'remove_duplicates should preserve order and remove duplicates'

testCases.description: remove_duplicates should return same list if no duplicates
testCases.validation: assert remove_duplicates([1, 2, 3]) == [1, 2, 3], 'remove_duplicates with no duplicates should return same list'

testCases.description: is_palindrome should handle mixed case palindromes
testCases.validation: assert is_palindrome('Race Car') == True, 'is_palindrome should handle mixed case like Race Car'

testCases.description: is_palindrome should return False for non-palindromes
testCases.validation: assert is_palindrome('hello') == False, 'is_palindrome("hello") should return False'

description: Practice identifying and fixing common Python bugs. Debug a function with a missing assignment operator (total + num vs total += num), fix a list-modification-during-iteration error in duplicate removal, and correct a palindrome checker that fails on mixed case and spaces. These are real-world bugs every Python developer encounters.
